data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) issued a virtual asset trading platform operating license to VDX, a subsidiary of Victory Digital; 2. OKX obtained a European payment license and plans to expand its stablecoin and cryptocurrency payment card business; 3. The crypto market's Fear & Greed Index rose to 12, still in the "extreme fear" range; 4. A newly created wallet withdrew 71.84 million SIREN from a CEX 10 days ago and currently has a floating profit of $8.98 million; 5. Trezor and Ledger users were targeted by physical mail scams that tricked them into scanning QR codes to steal their mnemonic phrases; 6. OpenEden's official website and portal DNS servers were suspected of being hacked; 7. Data: Russia's daily cryptocurrency trading volume has reached nearly $650 million; 8. Metaplanet released its 2025 financial report: holding 35,100 Bitcoins, aiming to control 1% of the supply by 2027; 9. Willy Woo: Quantum computing risks are weakening BTC's long-term valuation advantage over gold; 10. CoinShares: Global crypto ETPs saw a net outflow of $3.74 billion in the past month, marking four consecutive weeks of net outflows.

data.title: A whale deposited 3.16 million USDC into HyperLiquid and opened a limit buy order for XMR.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale deposited 3.16 million USDC into HyperLiquid and placed limit buy orders for XMR in the price range of $250-$315. Since January 15, the whale has purchased 7,189 XMR, worth approximately $3.59 million. These tokens have now been distributed to multiple wallets.

data.title: Bitcoin’s Quiet Buyers Absorb Record Supply During Market Dip
data.description:
Key Takeaways Accumulator addresses are buying at record levels, averaging about 372,000 BTC per month.
source: https://coindoo.com/bitcoins-quiet-buyers-absorb-record-supply-during-market-dip/

data.title: Hong Kong Pioneers Digital Yuan in Asset Settlement
data.description: Wang Yang, Vice President of the University of Hong Kong, announced that the city has begun experimenting with the digital yuan in its digital asset market settlement processes. According to NS3.AI, Hong Kong is also leading the issuance of digital bonds settled with both the digital yuan and the digital Hong Kong dollar. This initiative positions Hong Kong as a key player in mainland China's real-world asset markets and suggests the potential rise of offshore renminbi stablecoins supported by digital yuan reserves.

data.title: Logan Paul's Pikachu Illustrator Card Sets Auction Record
data.description:
A Pikachu Illustrator card owned by U.S. influencer Logan Paul has set a new auction record, selling for $16,492,000 at Goldin Auctions on February 16. According to BlockBeats, this card, considered the 'Holy Grail' of Pokémon collectibles, achieved a hammer price of $13.3 million, with an additional 24% buyer's premium bringing the total to over three times the amount Paul paid in 2021.

The card, which originated from a 1998 Pokémon illustration contest in Japan's CoroCoro Comic magazine, is one of only 39-41 ever awarded and was never publicly sold. It was hand-drawn by Pikachu's original designer, Atsuko Nishida, and features the exclusive 'Illustrator' designation. Logan Paul's card is the only known PSA 10 graded example, earning it the nickname 'Mona Lisa of the Pokémon world.'

The auction lasted 41 days and saw intense bidding, culminating in 97 bids during an extended bidding phase. Logan Paul attended the event, delivering the card along with a diamond-encrusted necklace he wore at WrestleMania 38 to the new owner, reportedly AJ Scaramucci, founder of Solari Capital and son of former White House Communications Director Anthony Scaramucci.

data.title: India's Trade Deficit Expands Ahead of U.S. Tariff Agreement
data.description: India's trade deficit saw an increase in January, occurring shortly before New Delhi reached a temporary agreement with the United States to reduce tariffs. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the timing of this economic development as significant for both nations. The interim pact aims to ease trade tensions and foster better economic relations between the two countries. This agreement is expected to have implications for various sectors, potentially influencing future trade dynamics. The widening deficit underscores the challenges India faces in balancing its trade relationships while pursuing economic growth.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Offers Support to Hungary Amid Financial Concerns
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is prepared to offer assistance to Hungary should it face financial difficulties, according to Secretary of State Marco Rubio. Bloomberg posted on X that Rubio made the statement during a joint press conference with Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban in Budapest. The announcement underscores the ongoing diplomatic relations between the United States and Hungary, highlighting potential economic collaboration. The discussion took place amid broader geopolitical considerations, with both nations expressing a commitment to strengthening ties and addressing mutual concerns.

data.title: Dogecoin's Price Rebound Amid Anticipation of X Money Integration
data.description: Discussions surrounding Dogecoin (DOGE) have intensified due to expectations of its integration into X Money, a new payment feature by Elon Musk's social platform X. According to NS3.AI, DOGE's price has experienced a short-term rebound, rising from $0.09 to over $0.11. Analysts are now targeting a move towards the 200-day moving average, estimated to be around $0.16–$0.17. Despite this price movement, institutional demand remains weak, as indicated by stagnant DOGE Spot ETF inflows. Additionally, the token's unlimited supply continues to challenge its long-term value retention.

data.title: Shiba Inu Price Hits Yearly Low Amid Lack of Token Burns
data.description: Shiba Inu (SHIB) experienced a significant price drop to $0.0000056 during the recent Valentine's Day weekend, marking its lowest point this year. According to NS3.AI, the absence of token burns during this period has contributed to the decline, as the burn rate has remained consistently low over the past year. This lack of token scarcity is affecting demand and raising concerns among investors about SHIB's potential for upward price movement. With anticipated network activity on Shibarium still weak, questions are emerging about SHIB's ability to achieve milestones such as reaching $0.01 without substantial token burns.

data.title: A Pokémon Pikachu Illustrator trading card sold for $16.492 million; the new buyer is reportedly the founder of Solari Capital.
data.description: A Pikachu Illustrator PSA 10 max-grade card sold at Goldin Auction House early this morning for a total of $16,492,000, setting multiple records. The final price was $13.3 million, and with a 24% buyer's premium, it reached $16.492 million. This is more than three times the price Logan Paul paid for it in 2021 for $5.275 million, and it also broke the Guinness World Record for the most expensive single card ever sold at auction, surpassing the previous record for sports cards. The new buyer is rumored to be AJ Scaramucci (son of former White House Communications Director Anthony Scaramucci), founder of the venture capital firm Solari Capital. (CNN)

data.title: A whale withdrew 578 BTC from Binance, worth $39.76 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 578 BTC from Binance, worth $39.76 million. The whale now holds 581 BTC, worth $39.76 million.

data.title: HKU Vice-Chancellor: Hong Kong's digital asset market has already experimented with the use of digital yuan in settlement.
data.description: In an article, Wang Yang, Vice-Chancellor of the University of Hong Kong, pointed out that the digital yuan has already been experimentally used in settlement in Hong Kong's digital asset market. The primary issuance of Hong Kong dollar and RMB bonds, in addition to traditional settlement methods, also marks the first time globally that digital bonds have applied digital yuan and digital Hong Kong dollars in the settlement process. Furthermore, Hong Kong will be the best market to absorb mainland assets through RWA (Retail Asset Exchange). With regulatory refinement and market development, a larger scale of RMB assets will have the opportunity to issue digital assets in the Hong Kong market, with digital yuan naturally being the preferred settlement currency. From a developmental perspective, stablecoins will become an important usage habit for users in overseas markets. Offshore RMB stablecoins are one of the future options in the internationalization of the RMB, and digital yuan deposits and digital yuan liquid assets provide the necessary reserve assets for offshore RMB stablecoins. (Hong Kong Wen Wei Po)

data.title: Gabler Group Plans IPO in Frankfurt
data.description: Gabler Group, a company specializing in submarine technology components, has announced its intention to launch an initial public offering (IPO) in Frankfurt. Bloomberg posted on X that the firm, which primarily generates revenue through defense contracts, is seeking to expand its financial base through this public offering. The move comes as Gabler Group aims to leverage its expertise in defense technology to attract investors and enhance its market presence. The IPO is expected to provide the company with the necessary capital to further develop its technological capabilities and strengthen its position in the defense sector.

data.title: Ether Rebounds Towards $2,000 Amid Market Pressure
data.description: Ether has shown resilience by recovering towards the $2,000 mark despite ongoing pressure in the cryptocurrency markets. According to NS3.AI, this recovery follows a substantial $540 million sell-off over the weekend. The rebound is occurring alongside stronger U.S. equity futures and rising gold prices, highlighting ether's relatively better performance compared to the broader crypto market amid prevailing uncertainty.

data.title: Burnout Memoirs Reflect Traditional Addiction Narratives
data.description: A new wave of burnout memoirs is emerging, drawing parallels to traditional addiction stories with vivid details and intense lows. Bloomberg posted on X, these narratives replace tales of wild nights with accounts of solitary all-nighters and anxiety-filled moments at the desk. The memoirs delve into the personal experiences of individuals grappling with burnout, highlighting the emotional and physical toll it takes. This trend underscores the growing recognition of burnout as a significant issue in modern work culture, prompting discussions about mental health and work-life balance.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Visits Hungary for Nuclear Cooperation Talks
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio arrived in Budapest on Monday to meet with Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban and his government. According to Jin10, the discussions centered around a civilian nuclear energy cooperation agreement strongly advocated by U.S. President Donald Trump. The agreement aims to enhance bilateral relations and promote energy collaboration between the two nations.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iran's Revolutionary Guard Conducts Naval Drills in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Navy has conducted military exercises in the Strait of Hormuz to assess its readiness against potential security and military threats. According to Jin10, the exercises are part of Iran's ongoing efforts to ensure its strategic capabilities in the region. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical waterway for global oil shipments, and any military activity in the area is closely monitored by international observers. The drills underscore Iran's focus on maintaining a strong defensive posture amid regional tensions.

data.title: Wells Fargo Customer Experiences Delayed Check Processing
data.description: A Wells Fargo customer faced a challenging situation after depositing a $10,000 check at an ATM without receiving a receipt. According to NS3.AI, the funds were unexpectedly put on hold, leaving the customer in uncertainty for weeks. Despite repeated attempts to resolve the issue, the bank did not provide any explanation for the delay. Eventually, Wells Fargo escalated the matter and released the funds approximately a month later, but the reasons for the prolonged hold remain undisclosed.

data.title: STOCKS | Analysts Increase Earnings Estimates for Software Stocks Amid AI Concerns
data.description: Analysts are raising their earnings forecasts for software stocks despite recent selloffs driven by concerns over AI disruption. Bloomberg posted on X that Goldman Sachs strategists have noted this trend, highlighting the resilience of the software sector in the face of technological challenges. The strategists believe that the potential of AI to transform industries is prompting analysts to adjust their expectations, even as market volatility persists. This adjustment reflects a broader optimism about the long-term impact of AI on software companies, suggesting that the sector may continue to thrive despite short-term fluctuations. The strategists emphasize that while AI presents challenges, it also offers significant opportunities for growth and innovation within the software industry. As analysts continue to revise their earnings estimates, the software sector remains a focal point for investors looking to capitalize on technological advancements.

data.title: A whale opened a 20x leveraged short position in BTC and increased its short position in ETH, currently showing a floating profit of $1.47 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale opened a 20x leveraged short position in BTC (position of 50 BTC) and increased its short position in ETH to 21,000 ETH, and is currently showing a floating profit of $1.47 million.
